question_text: A microwave oven heats food using electromagnetic radiation with a frequency of 2.45 × 10^9 Hz. Calculate the wavelength of the microwaves used. (Use the equation: wave speed = frequency × wavelength. The speed of all electromagnetic waves is 3 × 10^8 m/s)
explanation: Using the wave equation rearranged as wavelength = wave speed ÷ frequency, we get wavelength = (3 × 10^8) ÷ (2.45 × 10^9) = 0.122 m. This wavelength is typical for microwave ovens and is absorbed well by water molecules in food.
